seo.title: Instructor Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Instructor agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using Instructor for structured output extraction from LLMs face heightened risks due to stringent regulations like FedRAMP and FISMA, where unreliable AI outputs can lead to national security breaches or civil liberties violations. Compliance guardrails are essential to ensure validated Pydantic models prevent hallucinated data or incomplete extractions that could affect citizen-facing decisions. Vex provides runtime verification to enforce schema adherence, making Instructor deployments trustworthy in high-stakes government environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and validation of AI systems processing federal data to ensure reliability and security.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex wraps Instructor's client.chat.completions.create() with vex.verify() to validate structured outputs against Pydantic schemas at runtime, providing audit logs for FedRAMP compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk assessments and controls for AI systems, including output validation to prevent erroneous decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and retries common Instructor failures like partial extractions or hallucinated enums, ensuring consistent nested object depth per NIST validation controls.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires AI systems affecting citizens to be explainable, with validated outputs and appeal processes.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex generates verification traces explaining why outputs failed schema validation, enabling explainable AI decisions with Instructor-extracted models.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI-assisted outputs are accessible and reliably structured for public use.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces complete field extraction in Instructor models, preventing partial outputs that could violate accessibility standards.

industryRisks.name: National Security Breaches
industryRisks.description: Hallucinated enum values in classified data extraction leading to incorrect intelligence analysis.
industryRisks.example: Instructor extracts 'TOP_SECRET' instead of valid 'UNCLASSIFIED', triggering unauthorized access alerts.

industryRisks.name: Loss of Public Trust
industryRisks.description: Incomplete citizen benefit eligibility models causing wrongful denials.
industryRisks.example: Partial extraction misses 'income_threshold' field, denying valid applicants.

industryRisks.name: Congressional Investigations
industryRisks.description: Inconsistent nested object depths in policy impact assessments.
industryRisks.example: Government report models fail due to varying 'impact.regional' depth across responses.

industryRisks.name: Contract Debarment
industryRisks.description: Exhausted validation retries without fallback handling classified queries.
industryRisks.example: Instructor retries exhaust on malformed FOIA request extractions, halting processing.

implementation.title: Python: Secure Citizen Eligibility Extraction
implementation.description: Wrap Instructor's Pydantic patching with Vex verification to prevent partial field extraction in government benefits processing.
implementation.code:
from instructor import Instructor
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
import vex

class Eligibility(BaseModel):
    name: str
    income: float
    eligible: bool = Field(..., description="Must be True/False only")

client = Instructor.from_openai(OpenAI())

@ vex.verify(metric="government-eligibility")
def extract_eligibility(prompt: str):
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=Eligibility,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )

result = extract_eligibility("Citizen John Doe, income $25k, qualifies for aid?")
print(result.model_dump())

implementation.title: Python: Policy Impact Assessment

caseStudyScenario.heading: Instructor in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A federal agency deploys Instructor to process 10,000+ daily citizen inquiries about benefits eligibility, extracting structured Pydantic models from GPT-4o responses. Without verification, the system experiences frequent failures: 12% of extractions miss required 'eligibility_status' fields, 8% hallucinate invalid enum values like 'PENDING_REVIEW' instead of 'APPROVED/DECLINED', and nested 'dependents' objects vary in depth, causing database errors. During a congressional oversight hearing, malformed outputs lead to 300 wrongful benefit denials, triggering FISMA violations and public backlash.

The agency cannot process classified inquiries through external LLMs per Executive Order, but needs structured extraction for unclassified FOIA requests. Partial extractions exhaust retry limits, halting processing and risking contract debarment.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Implementing Vex guardrails reduced extraction failures by 98%, with automatic retries handling hallucinated enums and enforcing consistent nested depths. All 10,000+ daily extractions now validate against Pydantic schemas, generating audit trails for FedRAMP compliance. The agency avoided congressional investigation, restored public trust, and processed FOIA requests 3x faster with verified structured outputs, ensuring explainable decisions appealable to citizens.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with Instructor today to secure your government AI deployments against compliance failures.
